title: Boat Rental in Sabah Borneo: 2026 Costs, Routes and Boats
html:
Sabah Borneo offers some of Southeast Asia's richest cruising grounds, with trips from Kota Kinabalu to the Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park starting at €70 per person on a shared tour. Through BoatHire24, you can compare verified listings and book a charter online for a half-day or week-long voyage.

Where to set off in Sabah Borneo

The most common departure is from Kota Kinabalu, home to the Sutera Harbour Marina and the Jesselton Point Ferry Terminal. For longer expeditions, head to Sandakan Harbour (access to the Kinabatangan River and Turtle Islands) or the small port of Kudat on the northern tip. Both offer sheltered anchorages and access to local islands such as Manukan and Sapi. You can book a charter online in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Sabah Borneo?

Shared tours start at €70 per person, while a private half-day motorboat costs from €350. One option worth a look is the Princess F70 for hire.

Prices vary significantly by boat type and duration. A typical half-day charter for up to six guests runs €350, €500; a full-day catamaran for 12 people starts around €1,200. Crewed yachts with skipper and cook are available from €1,000 per day. For the most current rates, you can use the boat search to filter by boat type, capacity and budget.

  • Shared island-hopping tour: €70 per person, 4 hours
  • Small motorboat half-day (6 guests): €350, €500
  • Catamaran full-day (12 guests): €1,200, €1,800
  • Crewed yacht full-day (8 guests): €1,000, €1,500

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht, best for speed and comfort between islands, up to 10 guests, from €600 half-day. See the Princess F70 for hire on BoatHire24 for a luxury example.
  2. Sailing catamaran, ideal for stable, spacious cruising, up to 12 guests, from €1,200 full-day.
  3. RIB or sport boat, perfect for watersports and short hops, up to 6 guests, from €250 half-day.
  4. Traditional wooden boat, offers a cultural experience on the Kinabatangan River, up to 8 guests, from €300 half-day.
  5. Luxury yacht (Azimut, Fairline), for premium charter with crew, up to 10 guests, from €1,500 full-day.

What is the best season for boating in Sabah Borneo?

The best season runs from March to October, with calm seas and the southwest monsoon bringing reliable winds. You can also see more at check availability.

From November to February the northeast monsoon can bring stronger winds and rain, especially on the east coast. Local wind patterns such as the "land breeze" in the morning and "sea breeze" in the afternoon are consistent year-round. Always check the local forecast before departing.

Ask your rental operator about the "Sulu Sea swell", a gentle but persistent wave that builds in the afternoon, so aim to return to port by 15:00.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Sabah Borneo?

If you charter a crewed boat, no licence is required; bareboat rental may require a valid ICC or equivalent. To see how booking works, visit the boat search.

Sabah Borneo follows Malaysian maritime law, which generally requires an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) for bareboat charters. Most rental operators confirm exact requirements at the time of booking and often offer skippers for hire.

At a glance

  • Price band: €70, €2,000 per trip
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Trip length: 4 hours to 7 days
  • Max capacity: 2, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (local payments in MYR)

Questions about boat rental in Sabah Borneo

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Yes, for small motorboats under 15hp a licence is often waived, but confirm with the operator as policies vary.

What is the typical distance to the nearest island from Kota Kinabalu?

Gaya Island is only 3 nautical miles from Jesselton Point, a 15-minute ride.

Are there overnight charter options?

Yes, many catamarans and yachts offer overnight stays, with prices from €300 per person including meals.

Can I list my own boat for rent in Sabah Borneo?

Boat owners can register their boat on BoatHire24 and set their own schedule and rates.

To get started, explore the boat search on BoatHire24 and compare verified listings from local operators. For those looking to list their own vessel, check availability and become a host by visiting boathire24.com.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Sabah Borneo and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
